text:
CT SCAN OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
RESTAGING EXAMINATION

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Fifty-eight-year-old female with known Stage IV metastatic cutaneous melanoma, BRAF V600E-mutant, with metastatic involvement of bilateral axillary lymph nodes (left bulky), hepatic parenchyma, and subcutaneous soft tissues of the left flank. Patient has been on Tafinlar 150 mg twice daily and tram 2 mg once daily for approximately 8 months. Prior restaging CT at approximately 6 months demonstrated maintained partial response with significant reduction in nodal, hepatic, and subcutaneous disease burden. The current examination is performed to reassess disease status and evaluate for possible disease progression in the setting of rising LDH and new onset of left axillary pain reported by the patient.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 90 mL of nonionic iodinated intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350). Multiplanar reconstructions in the coronal and sagittal planes were obtained. Images are compared to the prior restaging CT performed approximately 6 weeks earlier as well as the baseline staging examination performed approximately 8 months ago.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidations are identified. No ground-glass opacities. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally. No pleural effusions. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinum is midline. No enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta and great vessels are unremarkable in caliber and enhancement.

Axillae: The left axillary lymph node conglomerate previously identified has clearly enlarged since the prior examination. The dominant left axillary lymph node now measures approximately 3.4 x 2.9 cm in the axial plane, compared to 1.8 cm at the 6-month restaging and 4.8 x 3.5 cm at baseline. The previously noted reduction in size has reversed, with the node returning toward its pre-treatment dimensions. A focus of central low-attenuation consistent with internal necrosis is again apparent within the dominant node, a feature not present on the prior 6-month scan. There is mild surrounding fat stranding. Right axillary lymph nodes remain small and unremarkable, the largest measuring approximately 0.8 cm in short axis.

Bone Windows (Chest): No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions are identified in the imaged thoracic skeleton.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. There is substantial interval increase in the size of the previously identified hypodense hepatic lesions. The dominant lesion in hepatic segment VI now measures approximately 2.6 x 2.2 cm, markedly enlarged compared to the prior measurement of 1.0 cm at the 6-month restaging scan (and a nadir of 1.1 cm at the 2-month restaging), though still smaller than the 3.2 x 2.8 cm seen at initial presentation. The lesion demonstrates peripheral heterogeneous enhancement with a relatively hypodense center on this portal venous phase acquisition, morphologically consistent with an actively growing melanoma deposit. Two additional hepatic lesions are newly identified since the prior examination: a hypoenhancing rounded nodule in segment II measuring approximately 1.5 x 1.3 cm, and a second discrete hypoenhancing focus in segment IVa measuring approximately 1.2 x 1.0 cm. These new lesions were not present on the 6-month restaging study. The previously near-resolved small lesions in segments IV and VII are no longer reliably differentiated from background parenchyma, though this may partly reflect their coalescence into the growing segment IVa lesion. No biliary ductal dilatation. The portal vein and hepatic veins are patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: The gallbladder is present without stones, wall thickening, or pericholecystic fluid. The common bile duct is not dilated.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances homogeneously throughout. No pancreatic masses or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: Bilateral adrenal glands appear unremarkable. No focal adrenal lesions.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement. No focal renal masses. No hydronephrosis bilaterally. No renal calculi.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Bowel loops are gas-filled and unremarkable throughout. No evidence of bowel obstruction or wall thickening. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

Peritoneum: No peritoneal implants or ascites identified.

Retroperitoneum: No enlarged retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph nodes. The aorta is normal in caliber.

Pelvis: Uterus and ovaries appear normal in size and morphology for age. No adnexal masses. No pelvic free fluid. Urinary bladder is normally distended without filling defects.

Lymph Nodes (Inguinal and Pelvic): A new right inguinal lymph node is identified measuring approximately 1.2 x 0.9 cm in short axis. This node was not present on the prior restaging study and is considered suspicious for metastatic involvement given the overall clinical context. Left inguinal lymph nodes are not enlarged. No pelvic lymphadenopathy.

Subcutaneous Soft Tissues: The previously identified subcutaneous nodule along the left lateral flank has re-enlarged. It now measures approximately 1.4 cm in greatest dimension, compared to 0.4 cm at the 6-month restaging scan and a nadir of 0.5-0.6 cm at 2 months. It is again well-defined and situated in the subcutaneous fat without deep fascial involvement. No additional new cutaneous or subcutaneous nodules are identified.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen/Pelvis): No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions in the visualized spine, pelvis, or proximal femora.

LABORATORY CORRELATION (PROVIDED WITH REQUISITION):
LDH reported as 285 U/L (elevated; institutional upper limit of normal 171 U/L), representing an increase from the normalized value of 160 U/L at the 6-month restaging.

IMPRESSION:

1. Clear-cut interval enlargement of the dominant left axillary lymph node, now measuring 3.4 cm (previously 1.8 cm at 6-month restaging), with recurrence of central necrosis. This pattern is distinctly worrisome for loss of treatment effect and resumption of active tumor growth, in sharp contrast to the impressive response seen on the 2- and 6-month restaging studies.

2. Significant increase in size of the dominant segment VI hepatic metastasis, now approximately 2.6 cm (previously 1.0 cm), accompanied by two newly apparent hepatic lesions in segment II (1.5 cm) and segment IVa (1.2 cm) not seen on the prior study. The emergence of new lesions alongside re-growth of established disease is highly concerning for acquired resistance to the current BRAF/MEK inhibitor regimen.

3. Re-enlargement of the left flank subcutaneous soft tissue nodule, now 1.4 cm, having nearly returned to its pre-treatment size after a period of near-resolution.

4. New right inguinal lymph node measuring 1.2 cm, not previously identified, representing a new anatomic site of suspected metastatic involvement.

5. LDH re-elevation to 285 U/L is noted and correlates with the radiographic picture of actively progressing disease.

6. Chest and brain remain without evidence of new metastatic involvement on the current examination (brain MRI performed separately and not included in this report).

Overall, the findings are those of multifocal disease progression involving the liver, left axilla, subcutaneous soft tissues, and now the right inguinal nodal region. The pattern — regrowth of previously responding lesions at multiple sites, combined with emergence of new hepatic deposits and a new nodal site — is consistent with acquired resistance to BRAF-directed therapy in this heavily pre-treated BRAF V600E-mutant metastatic melanoma patient. Clinical correlation and reassessment of the current treatment strategy is warranted.

REPORTING RADIOLOGIST: Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Division
EXAMINATION REVIEWED AND FINALIZED
